action.reasoning: Observation: Training produced NaN loss and very low val AP of 0.024 due to unmasked NaN labels propagating to loss and likely NaN weights in best.pt, causing eval to fail with no valid tasks (predictions contain nulls from NaNs). Previous good runs hit ~0.163 AP. Analysis: Root cause is BCE loss on NaN targets (no is_labeled mask), incomplete sklearn AP handling for all-nan/constant tasks, no grad clip; BN in model may exacerbate instability. FP caching works but pipeline broke on masking. Decision: Rewrite model.py to simple stable MLP (no BN), fully update train.py with masked loss (nan_to_num + is_labeled mean), robust per-task AP (filter positives), per-epoch jsonl logging, ReduceLROnPlateau, grad clip, 15 epochs; minor predict.py update for nan_to_num(0.5) safety. Train then submit_eval on best.pt to recover good AP and fix eval.
